name: Cuerda de los Morros Loop from Jayena
name_original: Cuerda de los Morros — circular desde Jayena — Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama
description: This challenging 24.8-mile mountain bike route in Sierras de Tejeda offers rugged terrain and panoramic views from the Alto de los Morros.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Jayena offer a diverse and challenging natural landscape, characterized by dramatic mountainous terrain and undulating hills. The region provides varied elevation changes, making it ideal for mountain biking. Jayena is situated within or in close proximity to the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, which features significant natural beauty and varied terrain. The trails include both paved and unpaved segments, catering to different riding experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Jayena The most popular mountain bike trail is Pico Lopera Loop Trail, a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain and elevation changes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cuerda de los Morros Loop from Jayena, a difficult 24.8 miles (40.0 km) path. This trail offers significant elevation gains and views of the surrounding natural park. Local mountain bikers also love the El Macho Marble Quarry – La Resinera loop from Jayena, a 33.1 miles (53.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Jayena is defined by mountainous terrain, rolling hills, and natural park landscapes, with trails featuring berms, rock faces, and jumps. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Jayena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 8 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Jayena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Jayena?
A: There are 15 mountain bike trails around Jayena listed on komoot. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from moderate to difficult, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Jayena?
A: The mountain bike trails in Jayena are generally challenging. There are 7 moderate trails and 8 difficult trails. Many routes feature significant elevation gains, requiring good to very good fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Jayena?
A: You can expect a diverse and challenging natural landscape, characterized by dramatic mountainous terrain and undulating hills. Trails often include both paved and unpaved segments, with features such as berms, rock faces, rock gardens, and jumps, providing a dynamic riding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Jayena?
A: Jayena offers year-round mountain biking due to its warm climate. Cooler months are ideal for lower-elevation routes, while the hotter months are more suitable for higher tracks and trails, especially those closer to the Sierra Nevada National Park.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers stunning views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5427076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Yeseras&lt;/a&gt; is a notable viewpoint. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013402164/cuerda-de-los-morros-loop-from-jayena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda de los Morros Loop from Jayena&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of the surrounding natural park, including the Alto de los Morros viewpoint.

Q: Can I find waterfalls or lakes on mountain bike routes in Jayena?
A: Yes, the area features beautiful water bodies. You can find the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/239989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Bermejales Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4443928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Funes Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. For waterfalls, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3522483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Chorreras Pool on the Río Verde&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight.

Q: What other natural features can I explore near Jayena's mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several mountain passes that offer challenging climbs and descents. Notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4353279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Blanquillo&lt;/a&gt; and the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1002788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carretera de la Cabra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Jayena?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 8 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse and challenging terrain, the significant elevation gains, and the stunning natural park landscapes that make for a rewarding riding experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Jayena?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Jayena are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017231613/pico-lopera-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Lopera Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17232201" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Macho Marble Quarry – La Resinera loop from Jayena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails in Jayena?
A: Trail lengths vary significantly. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017231613/pico-lopera-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Lopera Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is about 24.8 km (15.4 miles) and takes around 2 hours 31 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14596684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tambor Rest Area – La Resinera loop from Jayena&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 40.4 km (25.1 miles) and typically takes 3 hours 33 minutes.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails suitable for advanced riders seeking a challenge?
A: Absolutely. Jayena is well-suited for advanced riders. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013402164/cuerda-de-los-morros-loop-from-jayena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda de los Morros Loop from Jayena&lt;/a&gt;, at 40 km with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14594028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mesón Los Prados – Cerro Lopera loop from Morro de los Prados&lt;/a&gt;, which is 47.6 km with over 1400 meters of ascent, offer significant challenges.
tips: These half-destroyed forest huts are in the Sierra de la Almijara, Tejeda and Alhama, precisely in an area where there is a track that runs through the entire sierra or a large part of it, ending when the recinera arrives.
Viewpoint with panoramic views of the Sierras Tejeda, Almijara and Alhama Natural Park. A good place to take a break and enjoy the beautiful scenery.

Located in the municipality of Jayena, within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park (Sierra Sur of Granada).
Extensive pine forest with tables, barbecues, water fountains, showers, accessible restrooms, and a kiosk/bar, ample parking, and a dishwashing area. Camping and picnic areas are available amidst the natural surroundings.
Hiking: classic routes such as the "Poza del Romance" and the Los Ríos Botanical Trail are also ideal for families.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Cuerda de los Morros mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain bike route typically takes around 4 hours and 20 minutes to complete. However, given its difficulty and technical sections, it's wise to allow extra time for breaks and potential bike-pushing segments.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is not suitable for beginners. It is categorized as a 'difficult' mountain bike ride requiring 'very good fitness' and 'advanced' technical skills. Expect demanding terrain and sections that may require dismounting and pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route from Jayena?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Jayena serves as the starting point for this circular route. You'll likely find suitable parking within or on the outskirts of the village, which is a common access point for trails in the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the Cuerda de los Morros route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a rugged and varied terrain within the stunning Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. The landscape features sharp peaks, deep gorges, and extensive pine forests, along with holm oak and gall oak. The geological composition of limestone and dolomitic marbles gives the area unusual grey and white tones. Expect technical sections, potentially loose rock, and steep slopes characteristic of a challenging mountain bike ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is generally during the spring and autumn months. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter, which can make the already challenging terrain even more difficult.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or features along the Cuerda de los Morros trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the Monticana Forest Houses and can enjoy panoramic views from the Alto de los Morros Viewpoint. The El Bacal Recreation Area and Campsite is also along the way, offering a potential stop.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), Transandalus, Camino de la Plancha, Camino de Jayena a la Venta de los Prados, Camino de Jayena a la Cuesta de los Moros, Camino de la Cuesta de las Pulgas, and the Sendero del Bacal.

faqs.question: Is the Cuerda de los Morros trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on this challenging and technical mountain bike route. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for mountain biking in the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific entrance fees for accessing the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park for activities like mountain biking. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations for any specific permits required for certain activities or areas, especially if you plan to deviate from established routes or stay overnight.

faqs.question: What should I pack for mountain biking this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, pack essentials like plenty of water and snacks, a first-aid kit, bike repair tools (including spare tubes), appropriate protective gear (helmet, gloves), and navigation aids. Weather can change quickly in mountainous terrain, so layers of clothing are recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or stay near the trailhead in Jayena?
faqs.answer: Jayena is a small village that serves as a gateway to the natural park. You can typically find local cafes and restaurants offering traditional Spanish cuisine. For accommodation, there are usually rural guesthouses or small hotels available, providing a convenient base for exploring the area.

waypoints.name: El Bacal Recreation Area and Campsite
